vitaminD.name: Vitamin D Calculator
vitaminD.description: Find out how many minutes of sun you need daily to produce enough vitamin D.

meta.title: Vitamin D Calculator — Sun Exposure & Vitamin D Production
meta.description: Calculate your daily sun exposure for vitamin D synthesis. Based on skin type, latitude, season, and clothing — with seasonal UV chart.

vitaminD.title: Vitamin D & Sun Exposure Calculator
vitaminD.description: Find out exactly how many minutes of sun you need to produce 1,000 IU of vitamin D based on your skin type, location, and season.

skinTypes.0: Type I — Very fair, always burns, never tans
skinTypes.1: Type II — Fair, usually burns, sometimes tans
skinTypes.2: Type III — Medium, sometimes burns, gradually tans
skinTypes.3: Type IV — Olive, rarely burns, easily tans
skinTypes.4: Type V — Brown, very rarely burns, always tans
skinTypes.5: Type VI — Darkest, never burns, deeply pigmented

vitaminD.insufficientUV: The sun is too low or absent. UV-B radiation is insufficient for vitamin D synthesis at this time and location. Supplementation recommended.

statusDesc.deficient: Clinical vitamin D deficiency. See your doctor and start supplementation.
statusDesc.insufficient: Below optimal range. Regular supplementation recommended.
statusDesc.adequate: Normal range. Maintain with sun exposure or a maintenance dose.
statusDesc.optimal: Optimal level. Continue your current habits.

recs.high: Deficiency detected — 2,000–4,000 IU/day recommended (consult your doctor)
recs.sun: Current conditions support adequate vitamin D synthesis through sun exposure

vitaminD.seasonalDesc: UV-B potential at solar noon throughout the year for your latitude.
vitaminD.disclaimer: This calculator provides estimates for educational purposes only and is not medical advice. Vitamin D synthesis depends on many individual factors. Consult your doctor before starting supplementation, especially if you are deficient or have underlying health conditions.
